parents.subject: LU-10488 tests: saved and restore layout for dom test

commit.subject: LU-9937 llite: fix pio deadlock
commit.message:
LU-9937 llite: fix pio deadlock

When using pio, we take the inode lock in
ll_file_io_generic. Unfortunately, this violates the
ordering with lli_trunc_sem, as used in
vvp_io_setattr_{start/end}.

This can result in a deadlock.  This patch changes the
locking order between the inode lock and the trunc_sem,
because pio must hold the inode lock throughout an i/o, so
it cannot take it at the vvp layer.

Also clean up inode locking with pio, removing
lli_inode_locked. The inode info is shared between threads
doing i/o on that inode, so usage of this meant we would
not take the inode lock for operations that needed it if
someone already held it (notably ll_fsync), and we would
also let other threads unlock the inode lock for us,
possibly in the middle of operations which need it.

Instead, put a variable in the i/o to tell ll_fsync if we
have already locked the inode mutex.  The i/o is not shared
between threads, so should not result in mistimed unlocks.

Add a test for this and fix the existing pio test so it
really uses pio.

Signed-off-by: Patrick Farrell <paf@cray.com>
Change-Id: I6aac221942a92350850733ee3d3fe415b2be560a
